summary: The Docker build failed because a `wget` command inside the Dockerfile could not download the nydus binary from GitHub, receiving an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway error. This is a transient network failure that caused the `buildx bake` command to exit with code 1.
root_cause: A transient HTTP 502 Bad Gateway error from GitHub's servers caused the wget download of the nydus binary (https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/v2.4.0/nydus-static-v2.4.0-linux-amd64.tgz) to fail during the Docker build step, aborting the entire bake.

evidence.quote: #72 0.149 wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: This is the direct error message from wget showing a transient network failure when downloading the nydus binary from GitHub.

evidence.quote: #72 ERROR: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: This confirms the Docker build step failed due to the wget command exiting with code 1 after the 502 error.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: This is the final error reported by the GitHub Actions step, confirming the buildx bake failure was caused by the network error in the nydus download step.

evidence.quote:
Dockerfile:336
--------------------
 334 |     ARG TARGETARCH
 335 |     SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
 336 | >>> RUN wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz
evidence.reason: This pinpoints the exact Dockerfile line (336) where the failure occurred, confirming it is a network download step.

suggested_fix: Re-run the CI job, as this is a transient network failure (HTTP 502 from GitHub). Alternatively, consider adding retry logic to the wget command in Dockerfile line 336 (e.g., `wget --tries=3`) to make the build more resilient to transient network errors.
